gists.title: List gists for the authenticated user

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Only show results that were last updated after the given time. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

gists.descriptionHTML: Lists the authenticated user's gists or if called anonymously, this endpoint returns all public gists:

encoding.description: The encoding used for `content`. Currently, `"utf-8"` and `"base64"` are supported.

gists.title: Create a gist

bodyParameters.name: files
bodyParameters.description: Names and content for the files that make up the gist

childParamsGroups.name: key
childParamsGroups.description: A user-defined key to represent an item in files.

bodyParameters.name: public
bodyParameters.description: Flag indicating whether the gist is public

gists.descriptionHTML:
Allows you to add a new gist with one or more files.

Note

Don't name your files "gistfile" with a numerical suffix. This is the format of the automatic naming scheme that Gist uses internally.

encoding.description: The encoding used for `content`. Currently, `"utf-8"` and `"base64"` are supported.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

gists.title: List public gists

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Only show results that were last updated after the given time. This is a timestamp in ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

gists.descriptionHTML:
List public gists sorted by most recently updated to least recently updated.

Note: With pagination, you can fetch up to 3000 gists. For example, you can fetch 100 pages with 30 gists per page or 30 pages with 100 gists per page.

encoding.description: The encoding used for `content`. Currently, `"utf-8"` and `"base64"` are supported.

gists.title: Get a gist

parameters.name: gist_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the gist.

gists.descriptionHTML:
Gets a specified gist.

This endpoint supports the following custom media types. For more information, see "Media types."

  • application/vnd.github.raw+json: Returns the raw markdown. This is the default if you do not pass any specific media type.
  • application/vnd.github.base64+json: Returns the base64-encoded contents. This can be useful if your gist contains any invalid UTF-8 sequences.

encoding.description: The encoding used for `content`. Currently, `"utf-8"` and `"base64"` are supported.

gists.title: Update a gist

parameters.name: gist_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the gist.

bodyParameters.name: files
bodyParameters.description:
The gist files to be updated, renamed, or deleted. Each key must match the current filename (including extension) of the targeted gist file. For example: hello.py.

To delete a file, set the whole file to null. For example: hello.py : null. The file will also be deleted if the specified object does not contain at least one of content or filename.

childParamsGroups.name: key
childParamsGroups.description: A user-defined key to represent an item in files.

gists.descriptionHTML:
Allows you to update a gist's description and to update, delete, or rename gist files. Files from the previous version of the gist that aren't explicitly changed during an edit are unchanged.

At least one of description or files is required.

This endpoint supports the following custom media types. For more information, see "Media types."

  • application/vnd.github.raw+json: Returns the raw markdown. This is the default if you do not pass any specific media type.
  • application/vnd.github.base64+json: Returns the base64-encoded contents. This can be useful if your gist contains any invalid UTF-8 sequences.

encoding.description: The encoding used for `content`. Currently, `"utf-8"` and `"base64"` are supported.

comments.descriptionHTML:
Lists the comments on a gist.

This endpoint supports the following custom media types. For more information, see "Media types."

  • application/vnd.github.raw+json: Returns the raw markdown. This is the default if you do not pass any specific media type.
  • application/vnd.github.base64+json: Returns the base64-encoded contents. This can be useful if your gist contains any invalid UTF-8 sequences.
